name: Mirrored Index Assignment
pattern: The same off-by-one or transposed-index bug appears in several parallel code paths that were copied from a common template.
evidence_flow: One code location shows an indexing/bounds mistake; sibling locations sharing the same structure repeat the identical mistake.

name: Conflicting Source-of-Truth
pattern: Two modules each maintain their own copy of the same state or constant and can drift out of sync because neither defers to the other.
evidence_flow: One file defines a value; a second file redefines the same value independently, with no import or sync linking them.

name: Unhandled Error Path
pattern: A recurring failure mode (null, timeout, empty result) is handled in some call sites but silently ignored in structurally similar ones.
evidence_flow: One call site guards a failure case; a sibling call site invokes the same fallible operation without the guard.

name: Stale Cache Invalidation
pattern: Cached or memoized data is read after the underlying source changes, because an invalidation/refresh step is missing on one of the write paths.
evidence_flow: One write path invalidates the cache; another write path mutating the same source omits the invalidation.

name: Inconsistent Validation
pattern: The same input constraint is enforced at one boundary but skipped at another that accepts equivalent input, allowing invalid data through.
evidence_flow: One entry point validates an input shape; a parallel entry point accepting the same input shape performs no validation.

name: Resource Leak Pattern
pattern: A resource (file, connection, lock) is acquired and released correctly in some paths but leaked on an early-return or error branch that repeats across functions.
evidence_flow: One function releases an acquired resource on all paths; a structurally similar function leaks it on an early-return or exception path.
